what_it_does: A by-use acceptance gate (the lead's, #77 step c) to be executed on the LOCAL Supabase stack before the prod flip of the Claude Design connector. It defines what the gate proves, the preconditions to confirm, a four-step round-trip (A-D: box->shared publish, shared->box inject, CD->shared, shared->CD read), a five-part security bar (E-I: private stays dark, no impersonation/no service_role bypass, fail-closed tools, mandatory audit, internal lifeline intact), the pass condition (all boxes observed by use), pre-flip hardening items, and a note on FORM being out of scope for this backend gate.
observations: Insists 'No error is not a pass; the PURPOSE must be observed' — verification by use, real call + watched result, not by reading code

Lists concrete preconditions: local Supabase API/Auth :15421, DB :15432, 5 tables in supabase_realtime, boundary principal env (.boundary.env role=company_boundary), design_client credential (client_id=claude-design), seed 'design' shared channel, a control internal-only channel, fork's boundary Realtime sub connected with principal JWT in phx_join
Round-trip A-D uses checkbox items for each observable (row present, second session receives, poster not re-delivered/no echo, CD post reaches sessions, CD reads same data)

Security item F is flagged as THE crux: proves service_role does NOT bypass RLS on CD writes — a forged from_session must 403 through the Edge Function path, with a grep-corroboration that service_role touches only connector_audit + clients lookup
Item E proves private (shared=false) channels are never published to Supabase and CD gets 403 on internal or non-granted-shared channels
Item H: mandatory audit — every CD call writes a connector_audit row; an induced audit-write failure makes the call fail loud (no un-audited call succeeds)
Pass condition: ALL boxes by use; any red = not done, re-run the WHOLE gate not just the failed line (integration regressions hide)
Pre-flip hardening A (audit-BEFORE-execute) marked RESOLVED 2026-06-18 with the verification described; the 'audits AFTER dispatch' framing is called STALE

Pre-flip hardening B (audience binding) notes AUDIENCE defaults to generic 'authenticated', accepted for the first flip given scope+policy gates, with a custom-access-token-hook fast-follow to stamp aud=<MCP resource>
Note on FORM: this is a backend round-trip gate; the navigable/legible face is assessed when participation is live + visible, flagged so it is not skipped
standouts: Item F's named threat: service_role bypasses RLS, so a service_role-used CD write would silently succeed a forged from_session — the worst failure of a public connector — and the 403 proves it dead, corroborated by grep
The explicit STALE-correction on audit ordering (audit-BEFORE, not after)
conventions: Embodies the repo's verification-before-completion / no-green-paint discipline at the security boundary
Names the two RLS gates (posture + RLS) and ties each checkbox to an observable, not a code read
Carries provenance (authored by lead ch-al7jdfdr 2026-06-18 against live contracts) and cross-refs migration files by number
notes: The by-use security/round-trip acceptance gate that must go fully green on local before the connector's prod flip — proves CD is a scoped two-way peer with no impersonation, no service_role bypass, fail-closed tools, and mandatory audit.
questions: Was this gate ever run fully green on local, and is there a recorded result?
Did the audience-binding fast-follow (custom-access-token hook stamping aud) land before or after the public flip?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches. It states it is the by-use gate before the flip proving CD is a scoped secure peer on shared data, and the body delivers preconditions, round-trip, security bar, and pass condition to that exact end.
apparent_themes: verification by use / purpose-observed (no-error is not a pass)
security boundary: two-gate scoping (posture + RLS), deny-by-default, no service_role bypass
additive shared path must not disturb the live internal coordination fabric
intention_signals: Trying to prove, by real watched calls on the local stack, that opening Claude Design as a public two-way peer leaks nothing, cannot be impersonated, and audits everything — so the prod flip deploys a proven thing rather than new untested work.
novelty: The explicit service_role-bypass impersonation threat model for a public connector and the corroborate-by-grep requirement that service_role touches no CD-write path.
contribution: Contributes the executable by-use acceptance criteria for the Claude Design connector's security and round-trip — the preconditions, the A-D data round-trip, the E-I security bar with its named impersonation threat, the whole-gate-re-run rule, and the audit-before-execute and audience-binding hardening — a fused system would keep this as the connector's go-live gate.
summary_for_embedding: A by-use local-stack acceptance gate for the Claude Design connector: preconditions, an A-D data round-trip (publish/inject/CD-post/CD-read on shared channels), and an E-I security bar (private-stays-dark, no service_role-bypass impersonation, fail-closed tools, mandatory audit-before-execute, internal lifeline intact), all observed by real calls, with audit-ordering and audience-binding hardening notes.
intention_for_embedding: Written to prove on the local stack, before any prod flip, that the public Claude Design connector is a scoped two-way peer that leaks nothing, cannot be impersonated, fails closed, and audits every call — so the flip deploys a verified thing.
